acceptance_criteria.then: work contains only request-local fragments, decisions, plans, and evidence

requirements.object: transient work records from durable product requirements
requirements.rationale: A work item is an execution context and must not silently become the product specification.
requirements.subject: The repository
requirements.title: Work and specification boundary

verification.evidence: repository validation and skill contract tests

acceptance_criteria.given: a user expresses an incomplete or ambiguous need
acceptance_criteria.then: the agent presents the inferred goal, constraints, tension, and smallest consequential question without sycophancy
acceptance_criteria.when: different interpretations would change the outcome

requirements.object: the user's intended outcome through calibrated dialogue
requirements.rationale: Comfortable listening and precise articulation are necessary to find the real requirement without burdening the user.
requirements.subject: The development agent
requirements.title: Research-backed intent discovery

acceptance_criteria.then: it has one ID, subject, normalized action, object, status, acceptance criteria, verification, and traces in the canonical catalog

requirements.object: one atomic obligation per canonical requirement identifier
requirements.rationale: Atomic requirements support independent change, verification, and traceability.
requirements.subject: The requirements catalog
requirements.title: Atomic durable requirements

verification.evidence: schema validator rejects composite or incomplete obligations

acceptance_criteria.given: a change set names current catalog and item revisions
acceptance_criteria.then: the complete candidate validates before an atomic file replacement
acceptance_criteria.when: add, update, or retire is applied

acceptance_criteria.given: a delete is requested for a previously authoritative requirement
acceptance_criteria.then: the item remains as a retired tombstone with a reason

requirements.object: version-checked add, update, and retirement operations
requirements.rationale: Optimistic concurrency and tombstones prevent lost updates and erased history.
requirements.subject: The specification workflow
requirements.title: Safe requirement lifecycle changes

acceptance_criteria.then: human-readable requirement documentation is reproducible and byte-current
acceptance_criteria.when: documentation generation and drift checks run

requirements.object: human-readable requirement documentation from the canonical catalog
requirements.rationale: Generated views serve readers without creating a competing editable authority.
requirements.subject: The specification workflow
requirements.title: Generated requirements documentation

acceptance_criteria.then: router.py exposes orchestration and functions.py contains concrete processing

requirements.object: FastAPI operations into orchestration routers and concrete functions
requirements.rationale: A stable operation boundary makes flow derivation and detailed design inspection deterministic.
requirements.subject: The FastAPI implementation framework
requirements.title: FastAPI operation structure

acceptance_criteria.then: the generated Mermaid sequence preserves runtime call order and excludes decorators

requirements.object: operation sequence diagrams from FastAPI router syntax trees
requirements.rationale: Source-derived flow documentation stays aligned with implementation.
requirements.subject: The design generator
requirements.title: Router-derived sequence diagrams

acceptance_criteria.given: the application-produced OpenAPI document is available
acceptance_criteria.then: API, request, response, and schema documentation is generated without duplicate operation identifiers

requirements.object: API and interface catalogs from an OpenAPI document
requirements.rationale: OpenAPI is the executable interface source and avoids duplicated manual catalogs.
requirements.subject: The design generator
requirements.title: OpenAPI-derived interface design

acceptance_criteria.then: query objects and table CRUD relationships are generated from parsed SQL syntax trees

requirements.object: raw SQL into query-object and CRUD documentation
requirements.rationale: AST parsing provides structural evidence and rejects invalid SQL instead of guessing from text.
requirements.subject: The design generator
requirements.title: SQL-derived data design

verification.evidence: SQL AST fixture and CRUD output assertions

acceptance_criteria.given: an AWS CDK stack has synthesized a CloudFormation YAML or JSON template
acceptance_criteria.then: resource and parameter detail documentation is generated from that template

requirements.object: resource and parameter catalogs from synthesized CloudFormation
requirements.rationale: The synthesized deployment template is the concrete infrastructure interface.
requirements.subject: The design generator
requirements.title: CDK deployment design generation

acceptance_criteria.then: a source SHA-256 manifest exists and a clean regeneration is byte-identical

requirements.object: drift between implementation artifacts and generated detailed design
requirements.rationale: Digest and byte comparison enforce one-to-one reproducibility.
requirements.subject: The design workflow
requirements.title: Implementation-to-design drift detection

acceptance_criteria.then: every source has an official URL, version, checked date, and freshness interval

requirements.object: a versioned registry of SWEBOK and official cloud guidance
requirements.rationale: Versioned official sources make checklist claims reproducible while exposing stale guidance.
requirements.subject: The quality framework
requirements.title: Versioned standards source registry

acceptance_criteria.given: requirements, generated design, implementation, and tests are reviewed
acceptance_criteria.then: each selected checklist item records Pass with direct evidence, N/A with a scope reason, or Fail with an issue and retest method

requirements.object: delivery artifacts against applicable evidence-based checklists
requirements.rationale: A consistent evidence contract turns best-practice guidance into auditable quality gates.
requirements.subject: The quality workflow
requirements.title: Evidence-backed standards verification

verification.evidence: gate audit and standards registry tests

acceptance_criteria.given: the relevant skill folders are copied to a target repository
acceptance_criteria.then: ordinary chat triggers automatic bootstrap, requirements handling, design, implementation, tests, and publication without user-run commands
acceptance_criteria.when: a user describes a development need

requirements.rationale: The collection is a reusable reference, so users should not need to operate its internal machinery.
requirements.subject: The portable skill collection
requirements.title: Chat-first portable adoption

verification.evidence: profile installation and skill discovery tests
